name: eoq3pyecorempl

metadata.name: eoq3pyecorempl
metadata.summary: A model persistance layer for EOQ3 based on pyecore

documentation:
# eoq3pyecorempl - A model persistance layer for EOQ3 based on pyecore 

This is a model persitancy layer (MPL) based on pyecore. 
This MPL behaves similar to a WorkspaceDomain in EOQ2. 
A workspace directory containing M2 and M1 models in 

* <modelname>.ecore and <user model>.<modelname>
* *.xml

format is parsed and uploaded to a domain as a workspace model.
		
## Usage

Imports:

    from eoq3pyecorempl import PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl

Init and connect the MPL:

    mpl = PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl(WORKSPACE_PATH)
	
Load the workspace directory:

	mpl.Load()
	
Connect to the domain and upload the workspace model:

    mpl.Connect(domain)
	
	
Accessing a M2 model:

    res = domain.Do( Get(Cls("workspacemdbmodel__Resource").Sel(Pth("name").Equ("oaam.ecore"))) )
		
Accessing a M2 model:

	res = domain.Do( Get(Cls("workspacemdbmodel__Resource").Sel(Pth("name").Equ("MinimalFlightControl.oaam"))) )
	
Accessing the model content:

    res = domain.Do( Get(Cls("workspacemdbmodel__Resource").Sel(Pth("name").Equ("MinimalFlightControl.oaam")).Idx(0).Pth("content")) )
	
See also pyeoq/Test/Eoq3/test_pyecorempl.py for working code.

## Workspace

A valid WORKSPACE_PATH could look like

* Meta

  * oaam.ecore

* MinimalFlightControl.oaam
* Library.xml

Subfolders are not mandatory.

Such a workspace directory can be found at pyeoq/Test/Eoq3/testdata/Workspace.

## Documentation

For more information see EOQ3 documentation: https://eoq.gitlab.io/doc/eoq3/

## Author

2024 Bjoern Annighoefer

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__ResourceSetContent
documentation:
Sets the content of an resources without making a direct relation ship,
which would cause the link between EResources and content to break.
## RESOURCE CONTENT HELPERS ###

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__ObjectGetResource
documentation:
Retrieves the resource this eObject belongs to.
If it does not belong to any resource, None is returned

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__UpdateObjectLutFromUploadCmd
documentation:
Updates His entries in the internal object lookup table by Obj entries 
from a commands result. 
In addition the reverse look-up table is build

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__LinkResourceAndEResource
documentation: Establish a connection between workspace resources and pyecore resources

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__UnlinkResourceAndEResource
documentation: Release the connection between workspace resources and pyecore resources

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__GetModelsEResource
documentation:
Return the eResource for a workspace resource.
Only works if resource and eResource have been linked 
before.
If no eResource is linked, None is returned.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__UpdateThread
documentation: ## UPDATE THREAD ###

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__ProcessNextEvent
documentation:
Pops the next event from the event queue and processes it
Is thread-safe.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__ApplyDomainUpdates
documentation:
This applies crt, upd and del events 
received from the domain to the local models
and marks the workspace as dirty after changes

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__CrudCreate
documentation: ## INTERNAL CRUD INTERFACE USED FOR UPDATES ###

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__AddResource
documentation: ## UPDATE METHODS FOR FILE AND DIRECTORY CHANGES ####

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__RefreshResourceOrDirectoryPath
documentation:
Updates the last persistent path for this element and all children.
Should be called if a parent has obtained a new path.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__DeleteResourceOrDirectoryPath
documentation: Removes the last persistent path from this and all child elements

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SetObjectDirty
documentation: ## SAVE FUNCTIONS ###

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SetWorkspaceDirty
documentation:
Sets the MPL dirty. 
This marks it to be saved in the next save loop.
The time of setting dirty is remembered to calculate 
if the autosafe timeout is triggered.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SetWorkspaceClean
documentation:
Sets the MPL clean, i.e.
no saving of resources is required 
The time setting the MPL clean is rembered.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SaveWorkspace
documentation:
Persists the workspace model in the file system.
Changes are adapted as good as possible.
After saving the workspace is clean again.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SaveDirectory
documentation:
Save all resources in a directory and
all subdirectories.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SaveResource
documentation:
Saves a workspace resource.
Based on the content of the resource it is decided what
file format and save routine is used.
If the resource is detached from the workspace model, nothing will happen.

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SaveModelResource
documentation: Saves a model in the ECORE XMI format

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SaveXmlResource
documentation: Saves the plain XML files based on the XML model

methods.name: eoq3pyecorempl.pyecoreworkspacempl.PyEcoreWorkspaceMpl.__SaveXmlFindSubElems
documentation: A support function of __SaveXmlResource
